sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Manchester United are in poor form, losing their last three matches without scoring, while Aston Villa are on a strong run with three consecutive wins and clean sheets. This stark contrast suggests Villa's momentum could play a significant role.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Manchester United have not lost to Aston Villa, with a win and a draw. Historically, United dominate with 4 wins in the last 5 matchups, which may provide a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Aston Villa (6th, 66 points) have outperformed Manchester United (16th, 39 points) this season, scoring more consistently (58 goals vs 42). United's defense has been leaky (54 conceded), while Villa's attack, led by Ollie Watkins, could exploit this weakness.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Bruno Fernandes remains United's standout player with high ratings and creativity, but key defenders like Lisandro Martínez are missing. Villa miss Youri Tielemans, but Watkins and Morgan Rogers are in form and could capitalize on United’s defensive absences.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: United's home advantage at Old Trafford might help, but their inability to score recently and Villa's defensive solidity (3 clean sheets in a row) tilt the balance. Expect a tight game with Villa likely to edge it due to better current form.
